cases.workflow: Helix/Yazi escape handling without timing ambiguity
cases.reference: Kitty keyboard protocol maps Escape to CSI 27 u and the disambiguate flag sends Escape as CSI u.

cases.workflow: Shell/editor interrupt keys delivered to the application event loop
cases.reference: Kitty keyboard protocol uses lowercase Unicode key codes and encodes Ctrl as modifier value 5.

cases.workflow: Helix/Nushell shortcuts that collide with legacy Tab/Ctrl+I
cases.reference: Kitty keyboard protocol example encodes Ctrl+Shift+I as CSI 105;6 u.

cases.workflow: Zellij/Yazi bindings that otherwise look like the start of CSI
cases.reference: Kitty keyboard protocol calls out Alt+[ as a CSI ambiguity fixed by the disambiguate flag.

cases.workflow: Dead-key composition remains text input while Zellij enables enhanced keyboard disambiguation
cases.reference: IME/dead-key commits are text input transactions; the composing key must not be emitted separately or encoded as a Kitty keyboard event.

cases.workflow: Keypad navigation remains distinguishable from cursor arrows
cases.reference: Kitty keyboard protocol assigns keypad left its own private key code, CSI 57417 u.

cases.workflow: Keypad operators remain distinguishable from main-layout punctuation
cases.reference: Kitty keyboard protocol assigns keypad plus its own private key code, CSI 57413 u.

cases.workflow: Games and TUIs can distinguish key repeat while still receiving text
cases.reference: Kitty keyboard protocol reports repeat as event type 2 and associated text as code points when requested.

cases.workflow: Games and TUIs can distinguish release events
cases.reference: Kitty keyboard protocol reports release as event type 3 when report-event-types and report-all-keys are enabled.

cases.workflow: Text-aware editors receive both key identity and generated text
cases.reference: Kitty keyboard protocol example encodes Shift+A with associated text as CSI 97;2;65 u.

cases.workflow: Report-all mode exposes standalone modifier key events
cases.reference: Kitty keyboard protocol requires modifier key events to include the modifier state after the current event.
